COPIED
CORN Brand Identity · 2026 ART DIRECTION
Premium is what
you remove.
The complete brand identity system for Corn. Every color, typeface, and pixel specified to execution quality. The system is the brand.
VERSION 4.1 · Locked
STATUS Canonical
DATE March 2026
CATEGORY Premium Global Access Card
SCROLL
00 · DESIGN PRINCIPLE
The architecture is quiet.
The access speaks.
Every element earns its place. Confidence is communicated through precision, restraint, and the quality of what's offered. Never through decoration, volume, or spectacle. The brand operates at two speeds.
90%

Brand Foundation

The default register. Quiet, confident, architectural. Serif headlines, generous space, editorial photography, restrained gold. Landing page, the card, dining, hospitality, transportation, events, Instagram, email, press. This is the identity.

10%

Drop Mode

The activated register. Urgent, precise, scarcity-driven. Ticker bar, full-opacity gold, status badges, allocation data. Luxury goods drops, Signature Experiences, app drops section, X announcements, Telegram, Discord. The event within the brand.

01 · COLOR SYSTEM
Warm gold on
deep black.
Two anchors define the palette. Everything else supports them. Gold is the accent, never the surface. Black isn't true black. It's warm, with five distinct tiers for depth and hierarchy. Click any swatch to copy its value.

PAGE BACKGROUND

Panel #111111 Cards
Surface #161616 Elevated
Border #1E1E1E Dividers

Champagne #E8DCC8 Warm highlight
Secondary 55% opacity Body text
Alert #C45A5A Error · Sold out
Success #5AC47A Confirm · Active

Brand Foundation: Gold Restrained

12%
15%
60%

Gold at reduced opacity. Hairlines at 12%, subtle fills at 15%, muted accents at 60%. Used for section dividers, category labels, the italic serif accent. Never as a background fill. Never for body text. Gold whispers.

Drop Mode: Gold Activated

100%

Gold at full opacity. Solid fills for ticker bar, CTA buttons, LIVE badges. Gold borders at 2px for drop containers. The restraint of Brand Foundation makes this activation feel significant. If gold were always loud, these moments wouldn't land.

02 · TYPOGRAPHY
Serif where no one
expects serifs.
Three typefaces. Instrument Serif for display, the deliberate category dissonance that makes viewers stop. Barlow for everything functional. DM Mono for data. No other fonts. No exceptions.

03 · LOGO & IDENTITY
One mark. No container.
The primary brand mark is a dimensional sans-serif C monogram. The letterform uses Barlow 700 as a geometric base, with two key modifications: a 3D extruded edge treatment and diagonal terminal cuts on both ends. The wordmark is CORN in Barlow 600 with 0.22em tracking. No container on the mark — ever.
PRIMARY MARK · DIMENSIONAL C MONOGRAM
C C
PRIMARY · GOLD ON BLACK
Monogram Specifications
The primary brand mark is a dimensional sans-serif C monogram. The letterform uses Barlow 700 as a geometric base, with two key modifications:
3D extruded edge treatment: 2–3px offset at 135° angle
Diagonal terminal cuts on both ends of the C
Extrude color: #D4A843 at 20% opacity
Face color: #D4A843 at 100%
No container, no circle, no background shape
Minimum size: 24px digital, 8mm print
Alternate direction: Serif C with hairline ring (saved, not primary)
COLOR VARIATIONS
C C
GOLD ON BLACK
C C
GOLD ON PANEL
C C
BLACK ON GOLD
C C
WHITE ON BLACK
WORDMARK
CORN
Barlow 600 · 0.22em letter-spacing · Always uppercase · Warm White #F2EDE6 on dark · #050505 on light/gold · Never below 14px digital, 5mm print
LOCKUP VARIANTS
C C
CORN
HORIZONTAL LOCKUP
C C
CORN
VERTICAL LOCKUP
C C
MONOGRAM ONLY
IDENTITY SPECIFICATIONS
MARK
Dimensional C · Barlow 700 · 3D extrude at 135° · Diagonal terminal cuts
EXTRUDE
2–3px offset · #D4A843 at 20% opacity · 135° angle (bottom-right)
WORDMARK
CORN · Barlow 600 · 0.22em tracking · Always uppercase
DIVIDER
1px gold hairline at 25% opacity between monogram and wordmark in all lockups · Vertical: 40px wide · Horizontal: 32px tall
CLEAR SPACE
Minimum 0.5× mark height on all sides · 1× mark height between mark and wordmark in lockup
MIN SIZE
Monogram: 24px digital / 8mm print · Wordmark: 14px digital / 5mm print
ALT DIRECTION
Serif C with hairline ring · Saved, not primary · Do not use without approval
Do
Use the dimensional C as the primary mark everywhere
Maintain extrude offset proportional to mark size
Use monogram-only for small spaces (favicons, avatars)
Horizontal lockup for headers and nav
Maintain minimum clear space of 0.5× mark height
Don’t
Place the monogram inside a circle, square, or container
Use rounded corners on any container around the mark
Rotate, stretch, or distort the monogram
Use the serif C alternate without explicit approval
Place over complex photo backgrounds without darkening overlay
04 · CARD DESIGN
The object you
carry.
The physical card is the primary status signal — the object that represents membership. Matte black metal, sharp corners, and a single gold hairline along the bottom edge. The horizontal lockup sits top-left. VISA sits bottom-right. Nothing else. Every element earns its place.
PRIMARY CARD · FRONT FACE
CORN
VISA
CARD SPECIFICATIONS

Material

Matte black metal
ISO/IEC 7810 ID-1 (85.6 × 53.98mm)
Sharp corners in brand renders
Physical card: standard production radius

Surface

Background: #0E0E0E
Fractal noise texture overlay
25% opacity · overlay blend
No gloss · No reflective finish

Network

Visa network
100M+ merchants
200+ countries
Powered by Rain & Bridge

Lockup · Top-Left

Horizontal lockup
Position: 10% from top, 7% from left
Monogram: 40 × 45px
Divider: 1px gold, 37px tall, 25% opacity
Wordmark: Barlow 600, 17px, 0.22em tracking

VISA · Bottom-Right

Position: 9% from bottom, 7% from right
Barlow 700 · 18px
0.08em tracking
Warm White #F2EDE6 at 90% opacity

Gold Bottom Edge

Hairline: 1px, 90% opacity, edge fade
Ambient glow: radial, 70px reach
9% → 2.5% alpha
Bleed: 5px, 35% alpha, 2px blur

✓  DO
Use horizontal lockup (monogram + divider + wordmark) on card
Keep gold bottom glow subtle and ambient
Maintain fractal noise texture on card surface
Use sharp corners in all brand renders and mockups
Center card in compositions with generous surrounding space
✕  DON'T
Add cardholder name, card number, or expiry to brand renders
Use chip or contactless symbol in brand visual assets
Apply rounded corners in brand renders (production only)
Use the monogram alone on the card without the lockup
Place the card on light or colored backgrounds
05 · SOCIAL MEDIA
The brand in
every feed.
Social banners use the "access before the moment" photography treatment with the hero headline set in Instrument Serif. Avatars use the monogram only — no wordmark at avatar scale. Brand Foundation register applies to all profile assets. Drop Mode activates only in post content for drops and Signature Experiences.

PLATFORM DIMENSIONS
Platform Asset Dimensions Notes
X / Twitter Banner 1500 × 500 Safe zone: 150px from all edges
X / Twitter Avatar 400 × 400 Displays as circle — monogram only
Telegram Banner 1280 × 640 Taller ratio, headline can span 2–3 lines
CoinMarketCap Banner 1920 × 400 Ultra-wide, text in left 40%
Instagram Avatar 320 × 320 Displays as circle — monogram only
Discord Server Icon 512 × 512 Rounded square crop — monogram only
LinkedIn Banner 1128 × 191 Extreme wide — headline single-line only
OG Image Link Preview 1200 × 630 URL shares across all platforms
Social Rules
Banners: Brand photography with brand treatment filter
Headline: Instrument Serif, “Missing” always in gold italic
Avatars: Monogram only, gold on black, no wordmark
Profile assets: Brand Foundation register only
Post content: Drop Mode permitted for drops + Signature Experiences
Never
Use the wordmark as an avatar (too small to read)
Use untreated photography in banners
Place the full lockup over complex imagery
Use Drop Mode styling for profile/banner assets
Add taglines or URLs to banner images
06 · PHOTOGRAPHY
Access before the moment.
We show the in-between that only insiders see. The paddock before the race. The restaurant before service. The watch on the cloth, not on the wrist. Insider perspective, earned, not aspirational. Drag the slider to see the treatment.
BEFORE · UNTREATED
AFTER · BRAND FILTER
55%
BRIGHTNESS
65%
SATURATION
2.5%
FILM GRAIN
115%
CONTRAST

Subject Philosophy

"Access before the moment." The paddock before the race. The empty room at 5pm. The watch on the cloth, not on the wrist. Insider perspective that feels earned, not aspirational. Off-center composition, never centered subjects.

Hover State

On interaction, images reveal. Brightness lifts to 75%, saturation to 85%. The image breathes, as if the moment is about to begin. Transition: 0.6s ease on filter and transform. Scale: 1.02 on hover.

Aspect Ratios

Hero: 100vh full-bleed. Access cards: 4:5 portrait. Product/feature: 16:9 landscape. Negative space always on left for text overlay zones. Drop Mode: tighter crops, higher saturation (0.85 default).

07 · EDGE LANGUAGE
Sharp edges.
No exceptions.
Border-radius: 0 everywhere. Buttons, cards, inputs, badges, images, all sharp. Rounded corners signal consumer-friendly approachability. Sharp edges signal precision and architecture. This is a deliberate choice that separates Corn from every fintech and crypto card in market.
0px
SPACING SYSTEM · 8px BASE
44px
88px
1212px
1616px
2424px
3232px
4848px
6464px
9696px
120120px

Layout Padding

Desktop sections: 96–120px vertical, 64–80px horizontal. Cards: 24–32px interior. Mobile: 48–64px vertical, 24–32px horizontal. Premium equals generous space. Never compress to fit more. Cut content instead.

Component Gaps

Grid gap for card layouts: 2px (seamless tiling). Content gap for text blocks: 24px. Buttons: 16px between. Badges: 8px. Section to section: 0. Border-bottom handles separation.

Border System

Standard: 1px solid #1E1E1E for most dividers. Gold hairline: 1px rgba(212,168,67,0.12) for section headers. Gold solid: 1px #D4A843 for Drop Mode containers. Drop emphasis: 2px solid #D4A843.

09 · TWO REGISTERS
Quiet by default.
Urgent when it matters.
BRAND FOUNDATION
The things worth having aren't on shelves.
Quiet, confident, architectural. Serif headlines, generous space, editorial photography, restrained gold. This is how the brand looks 90% of the time.
GOLD AT 12–15% OPACITY
PHOTOGRAPHY: BRIGHTNESS 0.55 · SATURATE 0.65
BORDERS: 1px SOLID #1E1E1E
PACE: DELIBERATE · EDITORIAL
USE: LANDING PAGE · CARD · DINING · HOSPITALITY ·
EVENTS · INSTAGRAM · EMAIL · PRESS
DROP MODE · ACTIVE
Louis XIII
Le Jeroboam
Urgent, precise, scarcity-driven. Full-opacity gold, status badges, allocation data, ticker bar. The event within the brand.
GOLD AT 100% OPACITY
PHOTOGRAPHY: BRIGHTNESS 0.65 · SATURATE 0.85
BORDERS: 2px SOLID #D4A843
PACE: URGENT · TIME-BOUND
USE: LUXURY GOODS DROPS · SIGNATURE EXPERIENCES ·
APP DROPS · X ANNOUNCEMENTS · TELEGRAM · DISCORD
10 · MOTION SYSTEM
Smooth. Forward.
Never decorative.
Click any cell to replay the animation. Every motion has purpose: guiding attention, confirming interaction, or creating spatial continuity.
400
ms
Page Transition
ease-in-out · Slide left on navigate
Hero image parallax 20% slower
200
ms

ease-out · translateY(-4px)
Border-top color to gold · Immediate

ms

ms

ease · brightness + saturate

11 · RULES
What we do.
What we never do.
Do
Use Instrument Serif italic in gold as the brand signature
Let content breathe. Generous spacing signals premium
Use mono for all data, categories, and metadata
Keep photography desaturated with warm undertones
Use gold at 12% opacity for hairlines in Brand Foundation
Use 2px grid gaps for card layouts (seamless tiling)
Frame all borders and corners at 0 radius
Activate Drop Mode only for luxury goods and Signature Experiences
Let the contrast between quiet and loud create the impact
Use the "access before the moment" photo philosophy
Set DM Mono at 11px minimum (desktop), 12px (mobile)
Use gold serif at 32px+ on Panel backgrounds
Don't
Use Instrument Serif below 28px (32px on Panel backgrounds)
Use gold as a background fill. It's an accent, not a surface
Use Barlow at weight 300 below 16px on dark backgrounds
Use the italic for anything other than Instrument Serif in gold
Use rounded corners anywhere. No exceptions
Use Drop Mode components outside of drops context
Mix registers. Brand Foundation and Drop Mode don't overlap
Use pure white (#FFFFFF). Always warm white (#F2EDE6)
Use true black (#000000). Use #050505
Center subjects in photography. Off-center creates tension
Use DM Mono below 11px desktop / 12px mobile
Use tertiary text (35% opacity) for actionable content
12 · LOCKED DECISIONS
The system at
a glance.
DISPLAY
Instrument Serif · Regular + Italic · 28px min (32px on Panel) · Italic reserved for gold accent
WORKHORSE
Barlow · 400–700 · 12–24px · UI, body, nav, CTAs · Never italic
DATA
DM Mono · 400–500 · 11–12px · Categories, status, metadata · Always uppercase · 12px min mobile
GOLD
Warm Gold #D4A843 · Brand Foundation: 12–15% opacity · Drop Mode: 100%
TEXT
Warm White #F2EDE6 · Secondary 55% · Tertiary 35% · Never pure white #FFFFFF
BACKGROUND
#050505 → #0A0A0A → #111111 → #161616 → #1E1E1E · Never true black #000000
SPACING
8px base · Scale: 4 8 12 16 24 32 48 64 96 120 · Grid gap: 2px · Border-radius: 0 everywhere
PHOTOGRAPHY
Brightness 0.55 · Saturate 0.65 · Contrast 1.15 · Grain 2.5% · Warm undertone · "Access before the moment"
SIGNATURE
Instrument Serif Italic in #D4A843 · The ownable brand gesture · Display headlines only
MONOGRAM
Dimensional C · Barlow 700 · 3D extrude 2–3px at 135° · Gold #D4A843 · No container · 24px min
WORDMARK
CORN · Barlow 600 · 0.22em tracking · Always uppercase · Never italic
SOCIAL
Avatars: monogram only · Banners: brand treatment photography + Instrument Serif headline · Brand Foundation register for profile assets
REGISTERS
Brand Foundation (90%) + Drop Mode (10%) · Never mix · Drop Mode = luxury goods + Signature Experiences only
